subject: [PATCH RFC v2 00/18] mm/virtio: skip redundant zeroing of host-zeroed reported pages

reviews.author: Michael Tsirkin
reviews.summary:
Reviewer noted that implementing PG_zeroed check in folio_zero_user() would leak the page flag out of the buddy allocator, and suggested an alternative approach using ___put_folio(folio, gfp_t) to handle optional gfp flags on free

Reviewer noted that adding a gfp_t flag to indicate zeroing would abuse the existing gfp flags and suggested using optional gfp flags on free instead, which could be useful for certain callers.
reviews.raw_body:
I do not see how - this would require leaking the page flag out of the
buddy allocator.

---

But we do not have a gfp_t flag meaning "this has been zeroed"
and when I proposed something similar in v1, David hated abusing
gfp flags for what is not an allocation property.

reviews.author: Michael Tsirkin
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that replacing folio_zero_user() entirely is not feasible due to specific requirements of certain callers, such as hugetlbfs needing to zero its own pool, and suggested modifying the existing interface instead
reviews.raw_body:
We can't completely replace it with GFP_ZERO though e.g. because hugetlbfs
has its own pool and needs to zero that.

reviews.author: Gregory Price (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged Michael Tsirkin's feedback about propagating PG_zeroed bit and lifecycle problems, agreed that plugging __GFP_ZERO correctly is the feasible approach to avoid staleness issues.
reviews.raw_body:
Right, but you're leaking that bit of information out one way or another
- whether it's a page-flag or something else (pghint_t) you have the
same lifecycle problems (when does it become invalidated? how long can
it be trusted for?).

I suppose at least with (pghint_t) the data (in theory) falls out of
scope and doesn't live with the page - but guaranteed it just ends up
polluting more and more interfaces.

I'm seeing why David's suggest to plumb __GFP_ZERO correctly makes
sense, it's really the only feasible approach here that doesn't generate
a staleness problem with whatever information you try to leak out.

~Gregory

2026-04-21.patch_summary: This patch proposes to optimize the zeroing of host-zeroed pages in virtio by skipping redundant zeroing when the PG_zeroed bit is set. The approach involves adding a check in folio_zero_user() to skip zeroing but not cache flushing, and clearing the PG_zeroed bit. This change aims to reduce unnecessary work and improve performance.
